_meta.description: World Headlines Dashboard — curated RSS source list v2.0. 500+ sources, 45+ countries. Tier describes editorial relationship to power, not quality. Bias notes are descriptive not judgmental. RSS URLs marked verified:false should be checked before use.

tiers.state: Government-owned or directly state-controlled outlet. May reflect official narrative.
tiers.state-wire: Government-funded wire service. Generally professional standards but state-funded.
tiers.independent: Editorially independent from government. May carry owner, advertiser, or ideological bias.
tiers.exile: Founded in or relocated to another country due to press freedom conditions in home country.
tiers.foreign-funded: Funded by a foreign government. Editorially separated but funding relationship worth noting.
tiers.wire: Professional wire service supplying copy to other outlets. Generally fact-focused.
tiers.single-topic: Outlet covering one specific issue domain across all geographies.

_meta.bias_scale: left | left-center | center | right-center | right | nationalist | state-narrative
_meta.rsf_index: Reporters Without Borders Press Freedom Index rank for the outlet's home country. Lower is better. Updated annually.
_meta.cors_note: Most RSS feeds require a CORS proxy for browser-side fetch. Recommended: your own Cloudflare Worker proxy. Fallback: allorigins.win

national.name: Associated Press — Top News
national.ownership: Non-profit cooperative owned by member newspapers
national.note: Gold standard wire service. Feeds most US local papers.

national.name: Reuters — Top News
national.note: London-headquartered, globally trusted wire service.

national.name: NPR News
national.ownership: Non-profit, partially federally funded via CPB
national.note: Public radio. Federal funding is a small fraction of budget.

national.name: New York Times
national.ownership: New York Times Company (Sulzberger family controlled)
national.note: Agenda-setting paper. Paywalled articles but headlines free.

national.name: Washington Post
national.note: Historically investigative. DC political focus.

national.name: Wall Street Journal
national.note: News pages center-right. Opinion pages strongly conservative.

national.name: The Hill
national.note: DC-focused political coverage. Tries to run both sides.

national.name: Politico
national.note: DC insider publication. Heavily sourced from government officials.

national.name: Fox News
national.note: Dominant right-wing cable news. Opinion strongly shapes news framing.

national.name: Breitbart News
national.note: Nationalist-populist. Significant influence on conservative base.

national.name: The Atlantic
national.note: Long-form journalism and opinion. High editorial standards.

national.name: Vox
national.note: Explainer-focused journalism.

national.name: Mother Jones
national.note: Investigative, progressive. Strong on money in politics.

national.name: National Review
national.note: Flagship conservative magazine since 1955.

national.name: The Nation
national.note: Oldest continuously published weekly magazine in US. Progressive.

national.name: The Intercept
national.note: Investigative. Strong on national security and surveillance.

national.name: ProPublica
national.note: Pulitzer-winning investigative journalism. One of the best in the US.

national.name: The American Conservative
national.note: Paleoconservative. Anti-interventionist foreign policy. Distinct from mainstream right.

national.name: Jacobin
national.note: Democratic socialist. Strong labor and economics coverage.

national.name: Reason Magazine
national.note: Libertarian. Skeptical of both parties. Civil liberties focus.

national.name: Democracy Now!
national.note: Pacifica Radio originated. Strong international and labor coverage.

national.name: Axios
national.note: Smart brevity format. Strong on tech, DC, and business. Cox acquisition 2022.

national.name: Univision Noticias
national.note: Largest Spanish-language US TV network. Essential for Hispanic community news.

national.name: Telemundo Noticias
national.note: Second largest Spanish-language network. Strong immigration and Latin America coverage.

national.name: MinnPost
national.note: Minnesota non-profit journalism. Model for statewide accountability reporting.

national.name: VTDigger
national.note: Vermont statewide non-profit. One of the best state-level accountability operations.

national.name: Mississippi Free Press
national.note: Civil rights focused Mississippi journalism. Essential for Deep South accountability.

northeast.name: Pittsburgh Post-Gazette
northeast.note: Historically liberal, shifted right under Block family in recent years.

northeast.name: Manchester Union Leader
northeast.note: Influential in New Hampshire primary politics.

south.name: Miami Herald
south.note: Best Cuba and Latin America coverage of any US regional paper.

south.name: Tampa Bay Times
south.note: Owned by journalism school. Consistently well-funded investigative work.

south.name: Texas Tribune
south.note: Texas-only coverage. Strong investigative and data journalism.

south.name: Arkansas Democrat-Gazette
south.note: Last major US metro daily to print a daily paper.

midwest.name: Chicago Tribune
midwest.note: Historic paper hollowed out by hedge fund ownership cuts.

midwest.name: Chicago Sun-Times
midwest.note: Recently acquired by non-profit. Working class focus.

midwest.name: Block Club Chicago
midwest.note: Neighborhood-level Chicago coverage. Strong local accountability.

midwest.name: Des Moines Register
midwest.note: Key primary state paper. Political coverage outsized for market size.

west.name: Los Angeles Times
west.note: Major national paper with strong West Coast and Pacific coverage.

west.name: CalMatters
west.note: California policy and politics. Strong accountability journalism.

west.name: Seattle Times
west.note: Independently owned. Strong Boeing and tech industry coverage.

west.name: Denver Post
west.note: Reporters have publicly protested hedge fund ownership stripping newsroom.

west.name: Colorado Sun
west.note: Founded by Denver Post refugees. Strong model for sustainable local news.

west.name: Salt Lake Tribune
west.note: First major US daily to convert to non-profit.

west.name: Las Vegas Review-Journal
west.note: Ownership has pushed editorial line rightward.

west.name: Anchorage Daily News
west.note: Essential source for Alaska resource, indigenous, and Arctic coverage.

new_york.name: New York Post
new_york.note: Tabloid. Populist right. Major influence on local NYC discourse.

new_york.name: City & State NY
new_york.note: NY state and city politics focus.

los_angeles.name: LAist
los_angeles.ownership: KPCC / Public Media Group of Southern California (non-profit)

portland_metro.name: The Oregonian / OregonLive
portland_metro.note: Oregon's largest paper. Strong Portland metro and statewide coverage.

portland_metro.name: Portland Mercury
portland_metro.note: Portland alt-weekly. Culture, arts, progressive politics.

portland_metro.name: Portland Tribune / Pamplin Media
portland_metro.note: Parent company of most Washington County community papers.

portland_metro.name: Hillsboro News-Times
portland_metro.note: Primary newspaper serving Hillsboro and western Washington County, OR.

portland_metro.name: Beaverton Valley Times
portland_metro.note: Covers Beaverton, Aloha, and central Washington County, OR.

portland_metro.name: Hillsboro Herald
portland_metro.note: Community-written local news for Hillsboro. Intel campus, local government, immigration.

portland_metro.name: ValleyTimes.news (Washington County)
portland_metro.note: Covers Tigard, Tualatin, Sherwood, Newberg and south Washington County.

portland_metro.name: KGW8 Portland
portland_metro.note: NBC affiliate. Largest local TV news in Portland metro. Washington County desk.

portland_metro.name: KOIN6 Portland
portland_metro.note: CBS affiliate Portland. Washington County and statewide breaking news.

portland_metro.name: Oregon Capital Chronicle
portland_metro.note: Oregon state government accountability journalism. Non-profit.

miami_metro.name: Miami New Times
miami_metro.note: South Florida alt-weekly. Caribbean and Latin American diaspora coverage.

miami_metro.name: El Nuevo Herald
miami_metro.note: Spanish-language sister to Miami Herald. Essential for Cuban-American politics.

denver_metro.name: Colorado Sun
denver_metro.note: Reader-supported. Best model for surviving local news.

denver_metro.name: Westword Denver
denver_metro.note: Denver alt-weekly. Cannabis, local politics, culture.

seattle_metro.name: Seattle Times
seattle_metro.note: Independently owned. Strong Boeing, Amazon, and tech coverage.

seattle_metro.name: Seattle Met / PubliCola
seattle_metro.note: Seattle city politics accountability. City hall, housing, homelessness.

seattle_metro.name: The Stranger
seattle_metro.note: Seattle alt-weekly. Culture, local politics, LGBTQ+ coverage.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.note: Publicly funded via license fee. Editorial independence protected by charter but periodically challenged by government.

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.note: Non-profit structure insulates from commercial pressure. Progressive editorial line. Strong international coverage.

sources.name: The Times
sources.note: Historically prestigious. Editorially distinct from Murdoch tabloids.

sources.name: The Daily Telegraph
sources.note: Flagship right-wing broadsheet. Ownership change to UAE-backed fund raised editorial independence concerns.

sources.name: The Independent
sources.note: Digital-only since 2016. Saudi ownership via Lebedev is a noted editorial concern.

sources.name: Daily Mail
sources.ownership: Daily Mail and General Trust (Rothermere family)
sources.note: Largest UK newspaper by readership. Tabloid. Populist right. Significant political influence.

sources.name: The Mirror
sources.note: Working-class tabloid. Historically Labour-aligned.

sources.name: The Economist
sources.note: Classically liberal (free-market). Globally influential. UK-based but world scope.

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.note: Global financial paper of record. Strong geopolitics coverage.

sources.name: The Spectator
sources.note: Oldest weekly magazine in English. Conservative.

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.ownership: Xavier Niel, Matthieu Pigasse, Société des Rédacteurs
sources.note: Newspaper of record. Staff hold significant ownership stake protecting editorial independence.

sources.name: Le Figaro
sources.note: Defense contractor ownership is a noted concern for military/arms coverage.

sources.name: France 24 — English
sources.note: Funded by French state but editorially independent by charter. Strong Africa and Francophone world coverage.

sources.name: RFI — English
sources.note: Strong Africa coverage. French equivalent of BBC World Service.

sources.name: Mediapart
sources.note: Subscription investigative journalism. Broke multiple major French corruption stories.

sources.name: Der Spiegel
sources.note: Germany's most influential news magazine. Staff ownership protects editorial independence.

sources.name: DW (Deutsche Welle) — English
sources.note: German equivalent of BBC World Service. Strong Global South and Eastern Europe coverage.

sources.name: Handelsblatt
sources.note: Germany's Financial Times equivalent. Essential for EU economic policy.

sources.name: Bild
sources.note: Highest circulation paper in Germany. Populist tabloid.

_meta.name: Russia
_meta.press_freedom_note: Russia ranked near bottom of press freedom index. Independent media largely shut down or exiled since 2022. Use sources with this context in mind.

sources.name: TASS
sources.note: Official state wire. Shows what the Kremlin wants communicated nationally and internationally.

sources.name: RT (Russia Today)
sources.note: Explicitly aimed at international audiences. Used as soft-power instrument. Blocked in EU since 2022.

sources.name: Kommersant
sources.note: Business-focused daily. Historically more independent than other Russian outlets but ownership is oligarch-aligned. Self-censorship pressure significant post-2022.

sources.name: Meduza
sources.note: Relocated to Riga, Latvia in 2021. Designated 'undesirable organization' in Russia. Best independent Russian journalism available.

sources.name: The Moscow Times
sources.note: Relocated to Netherlands. English-language coverage of Russia from outside.

sources.name: Novaya Gazeta Europe
sources.note: Successor to Novaya Gazeta, which was suspended in Russia in 2022. Several staff have been murdered. Riga-based.

sources.name: iStories (Important Stories)
sources.note: Investigative journalism. Riga-based. Covers corruption and war crimes.

_meta.name: China
_meta.press_freedom_note: China ranked near bottom of press freedom. All domestic media operates under CCP oversight. Independent journalism inside China is not possible. Foreign journalist access severely restricted.

sources.name: Xinhua — English
sources.note: Official party wire service. Benchmark for what the CCP wants communicated to international audiences.

sources.name: People's Daily — English
sources.ownership: Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party
sources.note: Official party newspaper. Headline selection and framing directly reflects party priorities.

sources.name: Global Times
sources.note: More aggressive nationalist tone than official Xinhua. Often used as trial balloon for hawkish positions. English edition aimed at foreign audiences.

sources.name: CGTN — English
sources.note: China's international TV broadcaster. English-language global outreach arm.

sources.name: China Daily
sources.note: English-language soft power paper distributed internationally.

sources.name: Sixth Tone
sources.note: More nuanced domestic coverage than party outlets. Not fully independent but covers stories others won't. Best available window into domestic Chinese social issues.

sources.name: South China Morning Post
sources.note: Hong Kong-based. Historically credible. Alibaba acquisition in 2015 raised concerns. Broader range than mainland outlets but ownership creates CCP-adjacent pressure.

sources.name: RTHK (Radio Television Hong Kong)
sources.note: Historically editorially independent. Autonomy significantly curtailed since 2021 National Security Law.

sources.name: Radio Free Asia — China
sources.ownership: US Agency for Global Media (US government-funded)
sources.note: US-funded. Covers stories suppressed inside China including Xinjiang and Tibet. Funding source means US government editorial pressure is a legitimate concern.

sources.name: Caixin Global
sources.note: Financial investigative journalism. The most credibly independent domestic outlet. Has published stories embarrassing to the state. Partially paywalled.

_meta.name: Taiwan
_meta.note: Taiwan listed as a separate entry from China. This reflects its de facto independent government and press freedom. Sources here provide essential contrast to mainland Chinese state media.

sources.name: CNA (Central News Agency)
sources.note: Government-funded but professional editorial standards. Reuters-style wire output.

sources.name: Taipei Times
sources.note: English-language. Pro-Taiwan identity. Most accessible for non-Chinese readers.

sources.name: Want Want China Times
sources.ownership: Want Want Holdings (Tsai Eng-meng — major mainland China business interests)
sources.note: Pro-unification. Owner has extensive mainland business. Provides important contrast to independence-leaning Taiwan press.

sources.name: CommonWealth Magazine
sources.note: Business and policy focus. High quality investigative work.

_meta.name: Iran
_meta.press_freedom_note: One of world's most restrictive press environments. Dozens of journalists imprisoned. All licensed media operates under state oversight.

sources.name: IRNA — English
sources.note: Official state wire. Shows Islamic Republic's official positions.

sources.name: Press TV
sources.note: Iran's English-language international broadcaster. Sanctioned in US and EU.

sources.name: Iran International
sources.note: Credible breaking news on Iran. London-based. Funding connections to Saudi interests are a legitimate concern given Iran-Saudi rivalry. Staff have faced assassination threats from IRGC.

sources.name: Radio Farda
sources.ownership: US Agency for Global Media (US government)
sources.note: US-funded Persian service. Covers stories suppressed in Iran. US government funding is a noted bias concern.

sources.name: IranWire
sources.note: Citizen journalism and investigations. Founded by former Newsweek Iran journalist.

sources.name: Kyiv Independent
sources.note: Founded in 2021 by journalists who left Kyiv Post after editorial interference. Became essential war coverage source post-2022.

sources.name: Ukrainska Pravda
sources.note: Ukraine's most read news site. Editor Georgiy Gongadze was murdered in 2000. The paper survived and remained independent.

sources.name: Haaretz — English
sources.note: Israel's newspaper of record. Left-leaning. Critical of settlement policy. Most globally cited Israeli paper.

sources.name: Ynet
sources.note: Largest circulation Hebrew news site.

sources.name: Mondoweiss
sources.note: Pro-Palestinian perspective. Essential for understanding left-wing Jewish and pro-Palestinian narrative on the conflict.

sources.name: Ma'an News Agency
sources.note: Independent Palestinian news agency. West Bank based.

sources.name: The Electronic Intifada
sources.note: Pro-Palestinian advocacy journalism. Chicago-based. Essential for Palestinian civil society perspective.

sources.name: NHK World
sources.note: Japan's public broadcaster. Internationally respected. Government-funded.

sources.name: Japan Times
sources.note: Primary English-language Japanese newspaper.

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.note: Asia-focused business journalism. Also owns Financial Times.

sources.name: Asahi Shimbun
sources.note: Japan's most respected liberal newspaper. Critical of nationalism and remilitarization.

sources.name: Yomiuri Shimbun
sources.note: World's highest circulation newspaper. Conservative.

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.note: India's most internationally respected newspaper. Strong foreign policy coverage.

sources.name: Times of India
sources.ownership: Bennett Coleman and Company (Sahu Jain family)
sources.note: India's highest circulation English paper. Commercially oriented.

sources.name: NDTV
sources.note: Adani Group acquisition by BJP-aligned billionaire is a significant editorial independence concern.

sources.name: The Wire
sources.note: Investigative. Frequently targeted by Modi government. Covers stories mainstream Indian press avoids.

sources.name: The Print
sources.ownership: Shekhar Gupta (former editor-in-chief of Indian Express)
sources.note: Strong on national security and defense.

sources.name: Hankyoreh
sources.note: Founded in 1988 by journalists who left state-controlled press. Staff co-ownership model.

_meta.name: North Korea
_meta.press_freedom_note: No independent press exists. KCNA is the only legal news source. All information from inside North Korea passes through state filter.

sources.name: KCNA — English
sources.note: Only official news source. Provides direct window into regime self-presentation. May be inaccessible from some networks.

sources.name: NK News
sources.note: Most credible independent North Korea monitoring publication. Seoul-based.

sources.name: Daily NK
sources.note: Uses network of sources inside North Korea. Risk to sources is real.

sources.name: Arab News
sources.ownership: Saudi Research and Media Group (SRMG), state-adjacent
sources.note: English-language. Soft power outlet for Saudi government positions.

sources.name: Al-Arabiya
sources.note: Pan-Arab network often seen as Saudi counterweight to Al Jazeera.

sources.name: Hurriyet Daily News
sources.note: Largest English-language Turkish paper. Owner's alignment with AKP government creates editorial pressure.

sources.name: Daily Sabah
sources.note: Openly pro-government. Reflects AKP narrative.

sources.name: Cumhuriyet
sources.note: Oldest Turkish newspaper. Secular, opposition-leaning. Journalists have been imprisoned.

sources.name: Bianet
sources.note: Independent civil society media. Human rights and press freedom focus.

sources.name: TRT World
sources.note: Turkey's international broadcaster. Pro-government editorial line.

sources.name: Al-Ahram — English
sources.note: Oldest and most influential Arab newspaper. State-owned since 1960.

sources.name: Egypt Independent
sources.note: English-language. Limited independence under el-Sisi government.

sources.name: Mada Masr
sources.note: Best independent journalism in Egypt. Repeatedly raided by authorities. Journalists arrested. Operates under extreme pressure.

sources.name: Mail & Guardian
sources.ownership: M&G Media (Guardian Media Group holds stake)
sources.note: South Africa's premier investigative newspaper.

sources.name: Daily Maverick
sources.note: Reader-funded investigative. One of Africa's strongest investigative outlets.

sources.name: amaBhungane Centre for Investigative Journalism
sources.note: South Africa's dedicated investigative journalism non-profit. Broke Gupta state capture story.

sources.name: Premium Times
sources.note: Best investigative journalism in Nigeria.

sources.name: The Punch
sources.note: Nigeria's highest circulation newspaper.

sources.name: Nation Africa
sources.note: Largest media group in East Africa.

sources.name: Addis Standard
sources.note: Most credible independent Ethiopian journalism in English.

sources.name: Ethiopia Insight
sources.note: Analysis focused. Strong on Tigray conflict and Horn of Africa geopolitics.

sources.name: Folha de São Paulo
sources.note: Brazil's largest circulation newspaper.

sources.name: O Globo
sources.note: Most influential Brazilian media conglomerate.

sources.name: The Brazilian Report
sources.note: English-language Brazil coverage. Strong on politics and environment.

sources.name: Agência Pública
sources.note: Brazil's leading investigative non-profit. Strong Amazon and corruption coverage.

sources.name: UOL Notícias
sources.note: Brazil's most visited web portal. Aggregates national news.

sources.name: G1 (Globo Digital)
sources.note: Brazil's most visited news site. Digital arm of dominant Globo network.

sources.name: Carta Capital
sources.note: Progressive political weekly. Critical of agribusiness and conservative coalitions.

sources.name: Nexo Jornal
sources.note: Explainer journalism backed by São Paulo universities. High editorial standards.

sources.name: Valor Econômico
sources.note: Brazil's financial paper of record. Brazilian equivalent of Financial Times.

sources.name: The Intercept Brasil
sources.note: Broke the Lava Jato/Moro leaks (Vaza Jato) scandal in 2019.

sources.name: Brasil de Fato
sources.note: Movement journalism. Land reform, labor, indigenous rights.

sources.name: Estadão (Estado de São Paulo)
sources.note: Historic independent São Paulo paper. Second most important nationally after Folha.

sources.name: Metrópoles (São Paulo/Brasília)
sources.note: Fast-growing digital news portal. One of Brazil's most read sites.

sources.name: Estado de Minas
sources.note: Minas Gerais newspaper of record. Mining, Petrobras, politics.

sources.name: GaúchaZH / Zero Hora
sources.note: Southern Brazil's dominant news platform. Successor to Zero Hora. Agribusiness and gaucho culture.

sources.name: Correio do Povo (Porto Alegre)
sources.note: Oldest still-publishing daily in Brazil (1895).

sources.name: A Tarde (Salvador)
sources.note: Northeast's most influential paper. Afro-Brazilian culture, Carnival, oil industry.

sources.name: Diário de Pernambuco
sources.note: Oldest newspaper in Latin America still publishing (1825).

sources.name: NE10 / Jornal do Commercio (Recife)
sources.note: Largest Northeast Brazilian web portal.

sources.name: Diário do Nordeste (Fortaleza)
sources.note: Fortaleza daily. Ceará's largest circulation paper.

sources.name: Correio Braziliense
sources.note: Brasília daily. Capital politics, federal government, Supreme Court.

sources.name: Gazeta do Povo (Curitiba)
sources.note: Paraná's flagship paper. Agribusiness, evangelical politics, conservative editorial line.

sources.name: Amazônia Real
sources.note: Independent journalism on Amazon deforestation, indigenous rights, river communities.

sources.name: El Universal
sources.note: Oldest and most-visited Mexican newspaper. Centrist-right historically.

sources.name: La Jornada
sources.note: Flagship left newspaper. Pro-AMLO / pro-Morena. State advertising is primary revenue.

sources.name: Reforma
sources.ownership: Grupo Reforma (Junco de la Vega family)
sources.note: Most credible independent national paper. Strong investigative. Paywalled.

sources.name: Milenio
sources.note: National reach. Print, TV, radio. Nuevo León industrial ownership.

sources.name: Proceso
sources.note: Premier investigative weekly. Journalists face regular cartel and government threats.

sources.name: Animal Político
sources.note: Best data and fact-checking journalism in Mexico. Electoral and corruption focus.

sources.name: Expansión
sources.note: Mexico's leading business and economy magazine.

sources.name: Mexico News Daily
sources.note: English-language Mexico coverage for expats and international audience.

sources.name: Pie de Página
sources.note: Investigative journalism on human rights, migration, land conflict. High quality.

sources.name: Quinto Elemento Lab
sources.note: Data-driven investigative journalism. Corruption, environment, organized crime.

sources.name: InSight Crime — Mexico
sources.note: Best English-language organized crime reporting in Latin America.

sources.name: ZETA — Semanario (Tijuana)
sources.note: Founded 1980. Co-founders murdered for cartel coverage. Mexico's most endangered investigative weekly. Tijuana Cartel, corruption, border politics.

sources.name: El Sol de Tijuana
sources.note: Daily. Part of OEM chain (70 regional papers). General city news.

sources.name: Frontera Digital (Tijuana)
sources.note: Largest daily in Tijuana. Border crossings, maquiladoras, US-Mexico relations.

sources.name: Tijuana Press
sources.note: Digital-native Tijuana news.

sources.name: La Voz de la Frontera (Mexicali)
sources.note: Mexicali daily. BC state capital coverage.

sources.name: Noroeste (Sinaloa / Culiacán)
sources.note: Sinaloa's major newspaper. Essential for Sinaloa Cartel and drug trafficking coverage. Journalists have been murdered.

sources.name: El Informador (Guadalajara)
sources.note: Jalisco's most respected newspaper. Founded 1917.

sources.name: El Norte (Monterrey)
sources.note: Monterrey's flagship paper. Mexico's industrial capital. Paywalled.

sources.name: El Diario de Juárez
sources.note: Largest paper on Mexican side of El Paso border. Cartel violence, maquiladoras, femicide coverage. Journalists murdered.

sources.name: El Mañana (Nuevo Laredo)
sources.note: Border city paper. Northeast cartel territory. Self-censorship is extreme due to cartel threat.

sources.name: El Dictamen (Veracruz)
sources.note: Oldest newspaper in Mexico (1898). Port city. Gulf cartel territory.

sources.name: Noticias OAX (Oaxaca)
sources.note: Primary Oaxaca paper. Indigenous rights, land conflict, SNTE teachers union coverage.

sources.name: Diario de Yucatán
sources.note: Peninsula's flagship paper. Maya culture, tourism, Tren Maya project.

sources.name: El Imparcial (Sonora)
sources.note: Sonora's primary paper. Maquiladoras, Arizona border, copper mining.

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.note: Australia's public broadcaster. Editorially independent. Frequently in tension with conservative governments.

sources.name: The Australian
sources.note: Nationally influential. Strongly conservative editorial line.

sources.name: Crikey
sources.note: Subscriber-funded. Strong on media industry accountability.

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.note: Canada's national newspaper of record.

sources.name: Toronto Star
sources.note: Canada's largest circulation paper. Progressive.

sources.name: CBC News
sources.note: Canada's public broadcaster. Frequently targeted by conservatives as biased.

sources.name: La Presse (Quebec)
sources.note: Canada's most read French-language newspaper. Converted to non-profit.

sources.name: The Tyee (British Columbia)
sources.note: BC-focused independent journalism. Strong on resource industry.

sources.name: De Correspondent
sources.note: Pioneering reader-funded model. Ad-free, no breaking news. Influential in journalism circles globally.

sources.name: Follow the Money
sources.note: Financial and political corruption investigations.

sources.name: Dawn
sources.note: Pakistan's most respected English newspaper. Frequently pressured by military intelligence.

sources.name: Geo News
sources.note: Largest Urdu news network. Has been blocked multiple times by Pakistani authorities.

sources.name: Efecto Cocuyo
sources.note: Independent digital journalism under extreme government pressure.

sources.name: El Pitazo
sources.note: Digital native. Operates under severe government restrictions.

sources.name: Irrawaddy
sources.note: Founded in 1993 by Burmese exiles. Thailand-based. Best English-language Myanmar coverage.

sources.name: Myanmar Now
sources.note: Operates underground since 2021 coup. Essential coup resistance coverage.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — English
sources.note: Qatar-funded. Genuinely strong international journalism and significant editorial independence in practice, especially on conflicts Saudi Arabia and UAE want suppressed. Coverage of Israeli-Palestinian conflict and Muslim Brotherhood politics reflects Qatari government interests.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Arabic
sources.note: Arabic edition covers Arab world more aggressively than English edition.

sources.name: TOLOnews
sources.note: Afghanistan's most watched TV channel. Continues operating under Taliban. Self-censorship significant.

_meta.name: Indonesia
_meta.note: World's fourth most populous country. Press freedom has improved but remains fragile.

sources.name: The Jakarta Post
sources.note: Indonesia's leading English-language newspaper. Standard reference for foreign readers.

sources.name: Jakarta Globe
sources.note: English-language digital news. Business, politics, culture.

sources.name: Kompas
sources.note: Indonesia's most read and trusted national newspaper. Founded by Catholic intellectuals.

sources.name: Tempo (Indonesia)
sources.note: Indonesia's best investigative journalism. Has been suspended multiple times for critical coverage.

sources.name: Detik.com
sources.note: Highest traffic Indonesian news site. Breaking news focus.

sources.name: Tirto.id
sources.note: Fact-checking and investigative journalism. Strong on politics and social issues.

sources.name: BenarNews — Indonesia
sources.note: Covers security, politics, and human rights in Southeast Asia.

_meta.name: Vietnam
_meta.note: One-party state. All media operates under CPV oversight. Independent journalism is impossible domestically.

sources.name: VnExpress International
sources.note: Most read Vietnamese news site. English edition is most accessible internationally.

sources.name: Tuoi Tre News (Youth Newspaper)
sources.ownership: Ho Chi Minh City Communist Youth Union
sources.note: Second largest Vietnamese paper. Party youth organ. Less stiff than official outlets.

sources.name: Vietnam News
sources.note: Official English-language state newspaper.

sources.name: The Vietnamese Magazine
sources.note: US-based. Covers human rights, democracy, and civil society stories suppressed inside Vietnam.

sources.name: Bangkok Post
sources.note: Thailand's oldest English-language daily. Regional benchmark for SE Asia news.

sources.name: Prachatai
sources.ownership: Non-profit Foundation for Community Educational Media
sources.note: Most important independent Thai journalism. Human rights, political dissent, lèse-majesté coverage. Has been repeatedly prosecuted.

sources.name: Khaosod English
sources.note: English edition of major Thai language paper. Good for breaking news.

_meta.name: Philippines
_meta.note: English is an official language; major papers publish primarily in English.

sources.name: Rappler
sources.note: Led by Maria Ressa (Nobel Peace Prize 2021). Faced Duterte-era legal harassment. Best investigative Philippine journalism.

sources.name: Philippine Daily Inquirer
sources.note: Philippines' most widely read broadsheet.

sources.name: Manila Bulletin
sources.note: Conservative. Oldest English-language daily in the Philippines.

sources.name: PCIJ (Philippine Center for Investigative Journalism)
sources.note: Pioneer of investigative journalism in Southeast Asia.

sources.name: Malaysiakini
sources.note: Most important independent Malaysian media. Has faced government lawsuits and sedition charges. Survived 1MDB era.

sources.name: Free Malaysia Today
sources.note: Digital native. Strong on political news and investigative reporting.

sources.name: New Straits Times
sources.note: Historic flagship English daily. UMNO political establishment ties.

sources.name: Channel NewsAsia (CNA)
sources.note: Primary English news broadcaster. Regional reach across Southeast Asia.

sources.name: The Straits Times
sources.note: Singapore's newspaper of record. SPH is restructured but historically state-adjacent.

sources.name: Rice Media
sources.note: Independent Singaporean outlet covering social issues and youth culture.

_meta.name: Colombia
_meta.note: High journalist murder rate. Many reporters operate under cartel and paramilitary threat.

sources.name: El Tiempo
sources.ownership: Grupo Empresarial El Tiempo (Casa Editorial El Tiempo)
sources.note: Colombia's most widely read paper. Historically close to establishment. Luis Carlos Galán connection.

sources.name: El Espectador
sources.note: Second major national daily. Critically covered Escobar. Offices bombed 1989.

sources.name: Semana
sources.note: Influential political magazine. Ownership change brought editorial concerns.

sources.name: La Silla Vacía
sources.note: Colombia's best political journalism. Power mapping and congress tracking.

sources.name: InSight Crime — Colombia
sources.note: Essential for FARC, ELN, drug trafficking, and cartel coverage in Colombia.

sources.name: El Colombiano (Medellín)
sources.note: Antioquia's flagship paper. Medellín narco history, peace process coverage.

sources.name: La Tercera
sources.note: Chile's second most-read newspaper. Center-right.

sources.name: El Mercurio
sources.note: Chile's oldest major paper. Conservative. Edwards family connection to Pinochet-era is historical fact.

sources.name: CIPER Chile
sources.note: Chile's premier investigative journalism outlet. Corruption, business, political accountability.

sources.name: El Mostrador
sources.note: Digital-only. Chile's oldest online news outlet.

sources.name: Interferencia
sources.note: Independent investigative outlet. Human rights and memory from dictatorship era.

_meta.name: Peru
_meta.note: High political instability. Press freedom threatened by successive governments.

sources.name: El Comercio
sources.note: Peru's oldest and most-read newspaper. Dominant media conglomerate.

sources.name: La República
sources.note: Second major daily. Left-leaning, critical of political right.

sources.name: OjoPúblico (Peru)
sources.note: Peru's best investigative journalism. Corruption, narco-politics, environment. ICIJ member.

sources.name: IDL-Reporteros
sources.note: Investigative outlet. Broke Vladivideos scandal. Justice and corruption focus.

sources.name: El País
sources.note: Spain's newspaper of record. Strong Latin America coverage.

sources.name: La Vanguardia
sources.note: Barcelona-based. Catalonia focus. Key for understanding Spanish regional politics.

sources.name: El Confidencial
sources.note: Digital-native. Best investigative journalism in Spain. Broke Panama Papers angle.

sources.name: elDiario.es
sources.note: Reader-funded progressive outlet. Strong investigative capacity.

sources.name: La Repubblica
sources.note: Italy's most read newspaper. Center-left flagship. Fiat/Agnelli ownership is noted.

sources.name: Corriere della Sera
sources.note: Italy's historic newspaper of record. Milan-based.

sources.name: Il Sole 24 Ore
sources.note: Italy's financial and business paper. Owner is Italy's main employer federation.

sources.name: Il Fatto Quotidiano
sources.note: Anti-corruption investigative journalism. Populist but editorial independence strong.

sources.name: Gazeta Wyborcza
sources.note: Poland's most influential newspaper. Founded during 1989 Solidarity transition. Under sustained attack from PiS government 2015-2023.

sources.name: TVN24 / Fakty
sources.note: Poland's most watched private news channel. US ownership protected it from PiS media law.

sources.name: OKO.press
sources.note: Fact-checking and accountability journalism. Founded in response to PiS rule.

sources.name: Polityka
sources.note: Poland's most respected weekly. Staff majority-owned.

sources.name: Le Desk (Morocco)
sources.note: Morocco's most credible independent digital outlet. Business and political accountability.

sources.name: Tel Quel
sources.note: French-language weekly. Covers stories Moroccan Arabic press avoids. Has faced legal pressure.

sources.name: Hespress
sources.note: Most read Arabic-language Moroccan news site.

_meta.name: Ghana
_meta.note: One of Africa's freest press environments.

sources.name: Graphic Online (Ghana)
sources.note: Ghana's oldest and most-read paper. State-owned but broadly professional.

sources.name: Joy Online (Multimedia)
sources.note: West Africa's most respected private broadcaster.

sources.name: Dakar Actu
sources.note: One of Senegal's most read digital news sites.

sources.name: Enquête Plus (Senegal)
sources.note: Investigative-leaning Senegalese journalism.

_meta.description: Outlets that purport to cover the entire world with no single national focus. Includes global wire services, international broadcasters, and worldwide industry publications.

general.name: Reuters — World
general.note: The global wire of record. Feeds most newspapers worldwide.

general.name: AFP (Agence France-Presse)
general.note: Third largest global wire. Strong Africa and Francophone world coverage. Nominally independent under AFP Act.

general.name: BBC World Service
general.ownership: BBC (UK public, Foreign Office-funded World Service historically)

general.name: Foreign Policy
general.note: US foreign policy and international relations focus. Elite policy audience.

general.name: Foreign Affairs
general.note: US foreign policy establishment publication. Reflects CFR member perspectives.

general.name: Voice of America
general.note: US government funded. Editorially separated by USAGM firewall. Critics question independence.

general.name: The Conversation — World
general.note: Articles written by academics and researchers. High expertise, readable format.

general.name: Quartz
general.note: Global business and economics. Emerging markets focus.

general.name: Rest of World
general.note: Technology's impact in the Global South. Fills a major gap in Western tech journalism.

general.name: Global Voices
general.note: Aggregates and translates citizen journalism from underreported regions worldwide.

general.name: OCCRP (Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting Project)
general.note: Pan-European investigative consortium. Broke Panama Papers, Pandora Papers. Essential for transnational corruption.

general.name: Bellingcat
general.note: Open-source intelligence and geolocation investigations. Critical on Russian military and disinformation.

energy_and_fuel.name: OilPrice.com
energy_and_fuel.note: Daily oil, gas, and energy market news.

energy_and_fuel.name: S&P Global Platts
energy_and_fuel.note: Commodity pricing and energy markets. Industry standard.

energy_and_fuel.name: Carbon Brief
energy_and_fuel.note: Climate science and energy policy. Exceptional data journalism.

shipping_and_trade.name: Lloyd's List
shipping_and_trade.note: World's oldest continuously published journal. Maritime industry bible since 1734.

shipping_and_trade.name: FreightWaves
shipping_and_trade.note: Freight, logistics, and supply chain. Strong US focus but global scope.

shipping_and_trade.name: World Trade Organization News
shipping_and_trade.note: Official WTO press releases and trade dispute updates.

shipping_and_trade.name: UNCTAD News
shipping_and_trade.note: UN trade and development. Strong Global South trade statistics.

finance_and_markets.name: Bloomberg Markets
finance_and_markets.note: Financial markets of record. Requires Bloomberg terminal for full access but RSS has headlines.

finance_and_markets.name: Naked Capitalism
finance_and_markets.note: Heterodox economics and finance. Skeptical of Wall Street and mainstream economic consensus.

finance_and_markets.name: Zero Hedge
finance_and_markets.note: Contrarian financial commentary. Often first on market stress stories. Significant misinformation and conspiracy content mixed in. Use with caution.

finance_and_markets.name: IMF News
finance_and_markets.note: IMF press releases, country reports, and economic outlooks.

finance_and_markets.name: BIS (Bank for International Settlements)
finance_and_markets.note: Central bank coordination and monetary policy research.

technology.name: Wired
technology.note: Stronger on societal impact and policy than pure product coverage.

technology.name: Ars Technica
technology.note: Best technical depth of major tech publications.

technology.name: MIT Technology Review
technology.note: Research-grade technology journalism. AI, biotech, energy tech.

technology.name: Rest of World — Tech in the Global South
technology.note: Technology's impact outside the US/Europe bubble.

technology.name: Protocol (archived via 404 Media successor)
technology.note: 404 Media: journalist-owned, strong on platform accountability and tech industry labor.

defense_and_military.name: War on the Rocks
defense_and_military.note: Military strategy and national security analysis by practitioners. No advertising. Donations-funded.

defense_and_military.name: Jane's by S&P Global
defense_and_military.note: Defense intelligence bible. Subscription but RSS has headlines.

defense_and_military.name: IISS (International Institute for Strategic Studies)
defense_and_military.note: London think tank. Strategic and military analysis.

defense_and_military.name: Stimson Center
defense_and_military.note: Non-partisan security and arms control think tank.

defense_and_military.name: Arms Control Association
defense_and_military.note: Nuclear and conventional arms control monitoring.

food_and_agriculture.name: Agri-Pulse
food_and_agriculture.note: US agriculture policy focus but global commodity coverage.

food_and_agriculture.name: FAO News
food_and_agriculture.note: UN Food and Agriculture Organization. Food security and humanitarian data.

food_and_agriculture.name: WFP News
food_and_agriculture.note: World Food Programme. Famine and food crisis front lines.

food_and_agriculture.name: GRAIN
food_and_agriculture.note: Non-profit advocacy. Land rights, biodiversity, industrial agriculture critique.

travel_and_aviation.name: Skift
travel_and_aviation.note: Travel industry trade publication. Airlines, hotels, OTAs.

travel_and_aviation.name: The Points Guy
travel_and_aviation.note: Consumer travel and loyalty programs. Affiliate revenue model.

travel_and_aviation.name: Simple Flying
travel_and_aviation.note: Commercial aviation news. Rapidly grown to major aviation trade outlet.

health_and_medicine.name: STAT News
health_and_medicine.note: Best US health and biomedical journalism. Strong on pharma and FDA.

health_and_medicine.name: The BMJ
health_and_medicine.note: British Medical Journal. Peer-reviewed. Strong health policy positions.

health_and_medicine.name: WHO News
health_and_medicine.note: UN World Health Organization official releases.

health_and_medicine.name: Devex — Global Health
health_and_medicine.note: Global development and aid. Strong on health system funding.

health_and_medicine.name: MSF (Médecins Sans Frontières) Press Releases
health_and_medicine.note: Field dispatches from conflict and disaster zones. Unfiltered.

environment_and_climate.name: DeSmog
environment_and_climate.note: Climate disinformation and fossil fuel industry investigations.

environment_and_climate.name: Mongabay
environment_and_climate.note: Tropical forests, biodiversity, and wildlife. Exceptional field reporting in Global South.

human_rights_and_justice.name: Human Rights Watch
human_rights_and_justice.note: Major international human rights NGO. New York-based. Reports are primary sources on abuses worldwide.

human_rights_and_justice.name: ICJ (International Court of Justice) Press
human_rights_and_justice.note: Official ICJ press releases. Primary source on international legal proceedings.

human_rights_and_justice.name: Justice in Conflict
human_rights_and_justice.note: International criminal justice and transitional justice.

space_and_science.name: SpaceNews
space_and_science.note: Space industry trade publication. Commercial and government launches.

space_and_science.name: Nature News
space_and_science.note: World's most cited scientific journal. News section covers major research.

space_and_science.name: Science Magazine
space_and_science.ownership: American Association for the Advancement of Science

_meta.description: Outlets that cover one specific issue domain or ideological lens across all geographies. These are not country-stamped. They should be displayed in a dedicated filter layer.

antiwar_and_peace.name: Antiwar.com
antiwar_and_peace.note: Founded 1995. Aggregates news skeptical of US military intervention. Libertarian-right politically but functionally anti-interventionist across the spectrum. Essential counterweight to defense establishment sources.

antiwar_and_peace.name: Common Dreams
antiwar_and_peace.note: Progressive peace and justice coverage. Strong anti-war tradition.

antiwar_and_peace.name: Responsible Statecraft
antiwar_and_peace.note: Restraint-focused foreign policy. Backed by both Koch and Soros. Explicitly anti-empire from a realist perspective.

antiwar_and_peace.name: Peace News
antiwar_and_peace.note: Oldest pacifist publication in the English language.

investigative_and_accountability.name: ICIJ (International Consortium of Investigative Journalists)
investigative_and_accountability.note: Coordinated global investigations. Panama Papers, Pandora Papers, FinCEN Files.

investigative_and_accountability.name: OCCRP
investigative_and_accountability.note: Organized crime and corruption across Europe, Central Asia, Africa.

investigative_and_accountability.name: Source Material
investigative_and_accountability.note: Environment, finance, and power. UK-based global scope.

investigative_and_accountability.name: Forbidden Stories
investigative_and_accountability.note: Continues the work of murdered or imprisoned journalists. Pegasus Project.

media_criticism.name: FAIR (Fairness and Accuracy in Reporting)
media_criticism.note: Media criticism from the left. Identifies omissions and framing in mainstream US press.

media_criticism.name: Press Gazette
media_criticism.note: UK journalism industry coverage. Media business, press freedom.

media_criticism.name: Columbia Journalism Review
media_criticism.ownership: Columbia University Graduate School of Journalism
media_criticism.note: Premier journalism review. Media criticism and industry trends.

indigenous_and_frontline.name: Mongabay
indigenous_and_frontline.note: Indigenous land rights often intersect with conservation coverage here.

diaspora_and_minority_press.name: Ethnic Media Services
diaspora_and_minority_press.note: Aggregates US ethnic minority press. Covers communities invisible to mainstream press.

diaspora_and_minority_press.name: New American Media
diaspora_and_minority_press.note: Immigrant and ethnic community media in the US.

labor_and_workers.name: Labor Notes
labor_and_workers.note: Grassroots labor organizing. Essential for understanding worker movements mainstream press ignores.

labor_and_workers.name: Work in Progress (ILO)
labor_and_workers.note: UN labor organization. Global employment and labor standards.

religious_and_faith.name: Religion News Service
religious_and_faith.note: Neutral coverage of faith and religion globally.

data_and_statistics.name: Our World in Data
data_and_statistics.note: Global development data visualized. Not breaking news but essential context layer.

data_and_statistics.name: Statista News
data_and_statistics.note: Data and statistics across all topics.

data_and_statistics.name: ACLED (Armed Conflict Location & Event Data)
data_and_statistics.note: Real-time conflict data globally. Academic standard for tracking armed conflict.

cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.name: CoinDesk
cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.note: Industry standard crypto news. Sold by DCG to Bullish in 2023. Ownership creates editorial tension.

cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.name: Cointelegraph
cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.note: High volume. Second largest crypto news site globally. Some sensationalist tendencies.

cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.name: Decrypt
cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.note: More editorial restraint than Cointelegraph. Good on regulatory news and DeFi.

cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.name: The Block
cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.note: Best institutional and regulatory coverage. Paywalled deep dives but free headlines.

cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.name: Blockworks
cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.note: Institutional crypto and DeFi focus. Strong on stablecoins, policy, macro.

cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.name: Bitcoin Magazine
cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.note: Bitcoin-maximalist perspective. Important voice in the Bitcoin-specific community.

cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.name: CryptoSlate
cryptocurrency_and_digital_assets.note: Market data and news. More sober than Cointelegraph.

nuclear_energy_and_industry.name: World Nuclear News
nuclear_energy_and_industry.note: Daily nuclear power industry news. WNA is a pro-nuclear industry group, so coverage is pro-industry by orientation.

nuclear_energy_and_industry.name: NucNet
nuclear_energy_and_industry.note: Independent nuclear industry wire service. More neutral than WNA publications.

nuclear_energy_and_industry.name: Nuclear Engineering International
nuclear_energy_and_industry.note: Technical trade journal. Reactor technology, fuel cycle, small modular reactors.

nuclear_energy_and_industry.name: IAEA Newscenter
nuclear_energy_and_industry.note: Official IAEA news. Safeguards, safety standards, nuclear applications.

nuclear_energy_and_industry.name: American Nuclear Society News
nuclear_energy_and_industry.note: US nuclear professional society. Research, policy, and industry news.

nuclear_energy_and_industry.name: NRC (US Nuclear Regulatory Commission) News
nuclear_energy_and_industry.note: Official US nuclear regulator press releases. Licenses, safety events, hearings.

nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.name: Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists
nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.note: Publishes the Doomsday Clock. Authoritative on nuclear risk, arms control, disruptive technologies.

nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.name: NTI (Nuclear Threat Initiative)
nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.note: Bipartisan global security. Sam Nunn / Ted Turner founders. Nuclear, biological, radiological threat reduction.

nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.name: Arms Control Association
nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.note: Monthly Arms Control Today journal + breaking analysis. Definitive treaty tracker.

nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.name: Federation of American Scientists (FAS)
nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.note: Nuclear stockpile estimates, secrecy news, arms race analysis. Hans Kristensen nuclear notebook is essential.

nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.name: Restricted Data (Alex Wellerstein Blog)
nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.note: Historian of nuclear secrecy. Best single source on nuclear weapons history and policy.

nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.name: SIPRI (Stockholm International Peace Research Institute)
nuclear_weapons_and_nonproliferation.note: Authoritative arms data. Annual yearbook on nuclear arsenals and military spending is the global reference.

narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.name: InSight Crime
narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.note: Premier English-language organized crime journalism in Latin America. Cartels, FARC, MS-13.

narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.name: BorderReport
narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.note: US-Mexico border specific. Nexstar owned but essential for border crossing, cartel, and migration news.

narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.name: El Blog del Narco
narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.note: Raw cartel violence documentation. Extreme content. Anonymous operators face lethal risk. Journalism of last resort for cartel-controlled areas.

narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.name: Narco News Bulletin
narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.note: Drug war journalism from below. Focus on civil society and human cost.

narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.name: OCCRP — Latin America
narco_trafficking_and_organized_crime.note: Corruption and crime investigations across Latin America.

immigration_and_migration.name: Documented NY
immigration_and_migration.note: New York-based immigration journalism. ICE enforcement, policy, immigrant communities.

immigration_and_migration.name: Univision Noticias — Inmigración
immigration_and_migration.note: Spanish-language immigration coverage for US Hispanic community.

immigration_and_migration.name: UNHCR News
immigration_and_migration.note: Official UN refugee agency. Global displacement data, humanitarian operations.

immigration_and_migration.name: IOM (International Organization for Migration)
immigration_and_migration.note: Migration statistics and field operations. Missing migrant data.

immigration_and_migration.name: Refugees Deeply / New Humanitarian
immigration_and_migration.note: Essential for refugee crises. Formerly IRIN/OCHA. Field journalism from displacement zones.
